title: NT has the most charities per capita
body: 341.2 registered charities per 100,000 residents — well above the national average. Often driven by smaller community and religious groups.

title: Victorian Catholic Education Authority Limited is the largest charity by revenue
body: Reported $3428 million in total revenue (2023 AIS). Headquartered in VIC.

title: Government provides 49% of all charity revenue
body: In 2023, registered charities reported $85.4 billion in government revenue out of $174.3 billion total — a single dominant funding source for the sector.

title: Donations and bequests are only 7% of revenue
body: Charities reported $12.2 billion in donations and bequests against $174.3 billion total revenue. Services and government dwarf public giving.

title: 29% of charities ran a deficit in 2023
body: 14,834 registered charities that filed a 2023 Annual Information Statement reported expenses exceeding revenue. A single year's deficit isn't unusual, but persistent deficits can signal financial stress.

title: Highest reported employee-expense ratio: 50199%
body: Lamdeni School Inc reported employee expenses equal to 50199% of gross income. High ratios can be normal for service-delivery charities (hospitals, universities) but worth investigating for advocacy or grant-makers.

title: 60 large charities depend on government for 90%+ of revenue
body: Among charities reporting over $1M in income, 60 get more than 90% of their funding from government contracts and grants. Effectively service-delivery arms of the state.

title: The largest sector by count is "religion"
body: 18,046 charities (28% of all registered) advance this purpose. Charities can have multiple purposes — totals across sectors do not sum to 100%.

title: 3.7 million Australians volunteer for registered charities
body: Across the 2023 AIS reports, charities account for 3,666,084 volunteer relationships and 947,335 full-time-equivalent paid staff. Volunteers outnumber paid FTE roughly 4-to-1.

title: Social welfare charities outnumber religious by 0.8x
body: 14,336 charities list "advancing social or public welfare" as a purpose, vs 18,046 for "advancing religion" — though many religious organisations also list welfare as a secondary purpose.
